concerns: Step 1 risk: `npm create vite@latest` in-place will prompt interactively and may collide with existing files (CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, Input/, Output/). Scaffold into a temp directory then merge, or use `--template react-ts .` with care.
Step 4: Vite already defaults base to '/' — setting it explicitly is harmless but unnecessary. Just confirm the default rather than treating it as a required change.
Node v25.8.0 is bleeding-edge; if any Vite or React peer-dep warnings appear during install, note them but they are unlikely to be blocking.
